mnemonics: Final Means FINAL: Final Stops Inheritance Now
Final Lock: Prevents Override, Inheritance, and Reassignment
keyPoints: A final class cannot be extended (inherited from).
A final method cannot be overridden by subclasses.
A final variable is a constant; its value cannot be changed once assigned.
commonMistakes: Trying to extend a final class, which will cause a compile-time error.
Attempting to override a final method in a subclass, which will cause a compile-time error.
Trying to reassign a value to a final variable, which will cause a compile-time error.
